edges.mechanism: SVP binds the vCArG III motif in the FT promoter via its MADS domain to directly repress FT transcription

evidence.title: Role of SVP in the control of flowering time by ambient temperature in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Yoo SJ, Park SH, Hwang I, Lee JS, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP represses FT expression via direct binding to the vCArG III motif in the FT promoter
evidence.claim: SVP directly represses FT via vCArG III binding

edges.mechanism: FCA represses SVP; SVP expression is elevated in fca mutants

evidence.title: Role of SVP in the control of flowering time by ambient temperature in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Yoo SJ, Park SH, Hwang I, Lee JS, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP functions downstream from FCA and FVE within the thermosensory pathway and mediates temperature signaling
evidence.claim: FCA acts upstream of SVP to repress it

edges.mechanism: FVE represses SVP; SVP expression is elevated in fve mutants

evidence.title: Role of SVP in the control of flowering time by ambient temperature in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Yoo SJ, Park SH, Hwang I, Lee JS, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP functions downstream from FCA and FVE within the thermosensory pathway and mediates temperature signaling
evidence.claim: FVE acts upstream of SVP to repress it

edges.mechanism: SVP and FLC proteins form a complex that binds to the FT promoter at different CArG motifs

evidence.title: Role of SVP in the control of flowering time by ambient temperature in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Yoo SJ, Park SH, Hwang I, Lee JS, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP and FLC proteins may interact in a complex during temperature signaling
evidence.claim: SVP interacts with FLC to co-repress FT

edges.mechanism: Warm ambient temperature triggers rapid SVP protein degradation via the 26S proteasome pathway

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP protein is rapidly degraded by the 26S proteasome pathway in response to warm ambient temperatures
evidence.claim: Warm temperature induces SVP protein degradation

edges.mechanism: The FLM-beta isoform predominant at low temperature represses FT; FLM-delta predominant at high temperature does not repress

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLM-beta is the predominant splice isoform at a low ambient temperature (16 degC), while FLM-delta is abundant at a high ambient temperature (27 degC)
evidence.claim: FLM-beta represses FT while FLM-delta does not

edges.mechanism: SVP and FLM interact genetically to repress flowering; SVP-FLM-beta complex forms repressor

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP and FLM interact genetically to repress flowering at a range of ambient temperatures
evidence.claim: SVP forms repressor complex with FLM

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature alters FLM alternative splicing toward the non-repressive FLM-delta isoform

evidence.title: Temperature Induced Flowering in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Balasubramanian S, Weigel D
evidence.evidence_sentence: splicing patterns of several flowering time regulators including FLM are altered during thermally induced flowering
evidence.claim: Warm temperature shifts FLM splicing to reduce repressive isoform

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly binds FT promoter and activates expression at warm temperatures when H2A.Z is evicted

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 plays an important role in the activation of FT expression at warm ambient temperature
evidence.claim: PIF4 directly activates FT at warm temperature

edges.mechanism: H2A.Z-containing nucleosomes at FT TSS block transcription factor access; temperature-induced H2A.Z eviction enables FT activation

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: H2A.Z is evicted from the TSS-proximal region of FT at warm temperatures, enabling transcription factors such as CONSTANS (CO) and PHYTOCHROME INTERACTING FACTOR 4 (PIF4) to access the FT promoter
evidence.claim: H2A.Z nucleosomes repress FT by blocking TF access

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature induces eviction of H2A.Z-containing nucleosomes from thermo-responsive gene loci

evidence.title: Epigenetic regulation of thermomorphogenesis in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Hou Y, Yan Y, Cao X
evidence.evidence_sentence: High ambient temperature induce the eviction of H2A.Z-containing nucleosomes, which contributes to the formation of an open DNA structure, thus facilitating the binding of transcription factors

edges.mechanism: Evening complex (ELF3-ELF4-LUX) directly represses PIF4 and PIF5 expression

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: The evening complex, ELF3-ELF4-LUX, directly represses the expression of PIF4 and PIF5
evidence.claim: ELF3 represses PIF4 via evening complex

edges.mechanism: Evening complex including ELF3 directly represses PIF5 expression

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: The evening complex, ELF3-ELF4-LUX, directly represses the expression of PIF4 and PIF5

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature reduces evening complex chromatin binding and induces ELF3 deactivation/degradation

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Warm temperatures reduce the binding of the evening complex to chromatin and eventually promotes the expression of PIF4
evidence.claim: Warm temperature reduces ELF3 repressive activity

edges.mechanism: Active phyB promotes PIF4 degradation; slow thermal reversion at low temperatures increases PIF4 degradation

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Slow thermal reversion of phyB at low temperatures induces the degradation of CO and PIF4 proteins

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature accelerates phyB Pfr-to-Pr thermal reversion, reducing active phyB pool

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: The conversion from Pfr into Pr is accelerated at warmer temperatures
evidence.claim: Warm temperature inactivates phyB via thermal reversion

edges.mechanism: Slow phyB thermal reversion at low temperatures induces CO protein degradation

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Slow thermal reversion of phyB at low temperatures induces the degradation of CO and PIF4 proteins
evidence.claim: phyB promotes CO degradation at low temperature

edges.mechanism: GI inhibits PIF4 activity establishing a negative feedback loop in thermomorphogenesis

evidence.evidence_sentence: GI, in turn, inhibits PIF4, establishing a negative feedback loop

evidence.evidence_sentence: In pif4-2 mutants, GI transcript levels were decreased...particularly at night, and further reduced in pifQ

evidence.authors: Li H, Xue M, Zhang H, Zhao F, Li X, Yu S, Jiang D
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of ELF3, phyB, or HY5 resulted in increased GI transcript levels, especially at night

evidence.title: A warm temperature-released negative feedback loop fine-tunes PIF4-mediated thermomorphogenesis in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Li H, Xue M, Zhang H, Zhao F, Li X, Yu S, Jiang D
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of ELF3, phyB, or HY5 resulted in increased GI transcript levels, especially at night

evidence.evidence_sentence: REF6 and PIF4 synergistically activate the expression of thermo-responsive genes at warm ambient temperature, thereby participating in the regulation of thermomorphogenesis

evidence.authors: Kim W, Ahn HJ, Chiou T-J, Ahn JH

evidence.authors: Kim W, Ahn HJ, Chiou T-J, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: TSF expression was increased by 1.67-fold in p35S:miR399b plants and 4.5-fold in pho2 mutants at 23degC

evidence.title: The Role of the miR399-PHO2 Module in the Regulation of Flowering Time in Response to Different Ambient Temperatures in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Kim W, Ahn HJ, Chiou T-J, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Mature miR399 is more abundant in plants grown at 23degC than at 16degC

evidence.authors: Hou Y, Yan Y, Cao X
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 promotes high temperature-induced hypocotyl elongation by binding to the promoter region of YUC8 to activate YUC8 expression

evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH

edges.mechanism: CO protein activates FT transcription in late afternoon of long days

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: Primary activation of FT in the late afternoon of long day conditions is accomplished through the action of CO protein

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: Repression of CO expression during the morning...is mediated by the CYCLING DOF FACTOR (CDF) family of DOF domain transcription factors

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CDF transcription factors, in addition to their binding to the CO promoter during the morning, are able to bind to the FT promoter at the 5' proximal region

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CDF repression of CO is released in the afternoon through the action of a blue light induced complex composed of the GIGANTEA (GI) protein and the LOV domain blue-light photoreceptor E3 ubiquitin ligase FLAVIN BINDING, KELCH REPEAT, F-BOX 1 (FKF1)

edges.mechanism: GI forms complex with FKF1 that degrades CDFs in afternoon

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CDF repression of CO is released in the afternoon through the action of a blue light induced complex composed of the GIGANTEA (GI) protein and the LOV domain blue-light photoreceptor E3 ubiquitin ligase FLAVIN BINDING, KELCH REPEAT, F-BOX 1 (FKF1)

edges.mechanism: FKF1 stabilizes CO protein under blue light

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: Under blue light conditions, FKF1, in addition to its described role in CDF degradation, also stabilizes CO protein

edges.mechanism: COP1-SPA complex ubiquitinates CO for proteasomal degradation in darkness

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana

evidence.evidence_sentence: In darkness, the CONSTITUTIVE PHOTOMORPHOGENESIS 1 (COP1) and SUPPRESSOR OF PHYA-105s (SPA1) form a protein complex which actively degrades CO through ubiquitin mediated proteasomal degradation

edges.mechanism: ZTL with GI destabilizes CO protein in morning

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: Together with GI, ZTL directly destabilizes CO protein in the morning of long days

edges.mechanism: HOS1 degrades CO, preferentially in morning and under low temperature

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CO degradation by HOS1 is both time and temperature dependent, as HOS1 preferentially degrades CO during the morning, and CO degradation by HOS1 is increased under low temperature conditions

edges.mechanism: CIB1 directly binds E-box elements in FT promoter 5' proximal region

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CIB1 directly binds to the FT promoter via E-box elements in the 5' proximal region
